title: Epigram. Dialogue Between A Catholic Delegate And His Royal Highness The Duke Of Cumberland.
content:
Said his Highness to Ned,[1] with that grim face of his,
        "Why refuse us the Veto, dear Catholic Neddy?"
    "Because, Sir," said Ned, looking full in his phiz,
        "You're forbidding enough, in all conscience, already!"
meaning:
Thomas Moore's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Epigram. Dialogue Between A Catholic Delegate And His Royal Highness The Duke Of Cumberland."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
